journal_entry.reason: Payment by cheque to settle existing accounts payable liability

output.liability_increase_reason: No liabilities are being created or increased; accounts payable is being settled (decreased)
output.equity_increase_reason: No equity accounts are affected by this transaction
output.revenue_increase_reason: No revenue is being recognized in this transaction
output.asset_decrease_reason: Cash (bank account) decreases by $50 when the cheque is issued
output.dividend_decrease_reason: No dividend or drawing accounts are affected
output.expense_decrease_reason: No expenses are being reversed or reduced; this is a payment of an existing liability

output.asset_increase_reason: No assets increase. Cash decreases (credit side), not debit.
output.dividend_increase_reason: No dividends or owner withdrawals involved.
output.expense_increase_reason: No expenses incurred. This is settling an existing liability, not recognizing a new expense.
output.liability_decrease_reason: Accounts Payable decreases by $50 when settled. This is 1 debit line.

flags.aspect: settlement of accounts payable with cash

output_tax_specialist.reasoning: Settling accounts payable is a payment of an existing liability. No tax is mentioned in the transaction text. The original purchase that created the payable would have already recorded any applicable tax at that time.
